title: FNO explainability: Fourier mode importance and feature importance
method: Train FNO on SPY. Ablate each Fourier mode (zero it) and each cascade level (permute it). Report Spearman drop.
interpretation: Mode 0 (the lowest-frequency Fourier mode) is the most important: zeroing it drops Spearman from 0.369 to 0.138 (importance = 0.231). Higher modes (1, 2, 3) contribute minimally. V1 (the realized vol) is the most important cascade level: permuting it drops Spearman by 0.068. The FNO primarily relies on the lowest-frequency Fourier mode and the V1 cascade level. This transforms the FNO from a black box into a scientific tool.
